name: Basic Buttermilk Wheat
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tablespoons Apple Juice, bottled or canned
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the buttermilk, apple juice, brown sugar (or maple), salt, baking soda, and gluten in a large bowl until well combined.
2. Gradually add the whole-wheat flour to the mixture and stir until a shaggy dough forms.
3. Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
4. Place the dough in an oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise at room temperature for approximately 1 hour or until doubled in size.
5. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C) and lightly grease a baking sheet.
6. Punch down the risen dough and divide it into four equal portions.
7. Roll out each portion into a round loaf, place on the prepared baking sheet, and let them rise for another 20 minutes.
8. Brush the tops of the loaves with water or oil before baking for about 30-35 minutes until golden brown and crusty.
9. Turn off the oven and allow the bread to cool completely on a wire rack before slicing.
